title: Research delegated Yggdrasil location agents
description: Explore an upstream-compatible Yggdrasil extension in which a constrained node key K authorizes a gateway key G to advertise reachability and relay opaque packets for K without exporting K's private key or terminating K's end-to-end sessions. Compare this with a stock-protocol split-node/remote-signer design and determine whether delegation provides enough bandwidth, state, mobility, or sleep-cycle benefit to justify a Yggdrasil protocol change. Relate the proposal to yggdrasil-go issue #118 and frame it as a constrained-node location agent rather than gateway route origination.
design: Evaluate a purpose-limited certificate Sign_K(domain, K, G, capabilities, epoch, serial, expiry) plus a fresh Sign_G(binding hash, locator, sequence, expiry). Preserve K as the address owner and session endpoint. Analyze lookup-driven advertisement rather than global route flooding; forbid delegation chains; define direct-K precedence, multigateway conflict resolution, replay persistence, revocation leases, loop prevention, privacy leakage, fair queuing, delegated-key limits, and gateway compromise. Separate always/recently reachable location-agent behavior from any future store-and-forward mailbox protocol. First test whether an unmodified Yggdrasil split node or transport adapter can meet the requirement with less protocol work.
acceptance_criteria: Produce a concise design comparison of stock split-node participation versus delegated location agents; identify exact yggdrasil-go control-plane, lookup, path-binding, forwarding, and session changes; specify certificate and live-binding byte inputs and security invariants; model bandwidth/state costs for constrained LICHEN nodes and gateways; define an offline simulation demonstrating lookup, session establishment, mobility, stale delegation, multigateway, revocation, loop, and DoS behavior; document compatibility and rollout options; recommend proceed, prototype, or reject; prepare a community-facing proposal referencing issue #118, but do not post or open upstream issues without explicit authorization.
notes:
Community integration plan:

1. Learn the room before proposing: join Libera.Chat #yggdrasil, observe current technical discussion, read recent Ideas Discussions, philosophical/BREAKING issues, merged protocol PRs, CHANGELOG entries, and develop-branch conventions. Use a short personal introduction: Mark, LICHEN, constrained LoRa nodes, preserving Yggdrasil end-to-end identity. Do not begin with a large design dump.

2. Establish the problem as general Yggdrasil work: constrained, mobile, and intermittently attached nodes need identity-preserving reachability through a better-connected peer. Present LICHEN as evidence and a simulation/test platform, not as a request for a project-specific gateway exception. Use 'delegated location agent' or 'constrained-node attachment', not BGP-like route delegation or gateway impersonation.

3. Ask maintainers informally on IRC whether the stock split-node/remote-signer approach has already been attempted and whether delegated location agents fit current routing direction. Ask which maintainer wants to guide the work and whether issue #118 should be continued or a focused issue opened. Treat IRC as reconnaissance only. Summarize every material answer and decision in the durable GitHub thread.

4. Open a GitHub Ideas Discussion only after the short IRC check. The post should contain: problem statement; current 0300::/8 routed-subnet limitation; invariants (K retains its private key, address, and session endpoint); non-goals (no key custody, impersonation, global route flood, delegation chains, or mailbox semantics); stock-protocol alternative; certificate/live-binding sketch; expected state and bandwidth; security failures; compatibility options; and specific questions for maintainers. Reference #118 and the radio-WAN Discussion #1284 without hijacking either thread. Any post/comment is an upstream write and requires Mark's explicit authorization at that time.

5. Convert feedback into a versioned Markdown design in the LICHEN repository or an agreed upstream location. Because Yggdrasil has no formal RFC/YEP process, make this artifact RFC-like: terminology, packet/state machines, canonical signed bytes, invariants, threat model, state/bandwidth model, migration, wire-version impact, test vectors, and rejected alternatives. Link revisions from the Discussion; do not leave normative decisions only in IRC.

6. Produce evidence before requesting core changes: implement the least-invasive stock split-node experiment first; run offline simulations for lookup, session setup, mobility, sleep/wake, stale delegation, multigateway selection, revocation, loops, state exhaustion, and gateway compromise; publish reproducible measurements comparing baseline and location-agent designs. Clearly separate location service from any future store-and-forward mailbox protocol.

7. Seek an explicit maintainer direction checkpoint: reject, continue simulation, prototype in a fork, or draft a protocol PR. If maintainers prefer another abstraction, update LICHEN rather than defending terminology. Do not submit a broad implementation PR before this checkpoint.

8. Prototype narrowly against yggdrasil-go develop in a dedicated fork branch: feature-gated/experimental where possible, bounded delegated state, no default experimental wire value, full tests/fuzzing, and no unrelated refactors. Track develop and current wire version. Demonstrate mixed-version behavior and whether a new incompatible handshake version is required.

9. Upstream in reviewable slices after maintainer invitation: protocol types/parsers and vectors; routing/path lookup behavior; forwarding/tunnel behavior; resource controls/observability; documentation and CHANGELOG. Follow maintainer preference if they request a single coordinated breaking-change branch instead. Every PR, issue, Discussion, or IRC commitment remains a human-authorized upstream action.

10. Maintain the relationship: answer review promptly, test maintainer alternatives, report negative results, keep LICHEN-specific policy out of Yggdrasil core, and contribute small independent fixes discovered during research only when separately scoped and authorized. Success means an informed upstream decision and reusable evidence, not necessarily acceptance of the original design.

Community deliverables: IRC question brief; GitHub Ideas draft; #118 relationship recommendation; RFC-like design; reproducible simulation report; maintainer checkpoint record; prototype/PR plan; durable summaries of IRC decisions.
